channel.title: NHC Eastern North Pacific
channel.description: Active tropical cyclones in the Eastern North Pacific

item.title: Eastern North Pacific Tropical Weather Outlook
item.description:
000
ABPZ20 KNHC 201140
TWOEP

Tropical Weather Outlook
NWS National Hurricane Center Miami FL
500 AM PDT Mon Jul 20 2026

For the eastern and central North Pacific east of 180 longitude:

Active Systems:
The National Hurricane Center is issuing advisories on Tropical
Storm Elida, located well west of the southern tip of the Baja
California Peninsula, and on Tropical Storm Fausto, located
several hundred miles west-southwest of southwestern Mexico.

Eastern and Central East Pacific:
A broad area of low pressure is forecast to develop well south of
Mexico by midweek. Environmental conditions appear conducive for
development of the system thereafter, and a tropical depression
could form while it moves westward to west-northwestward across the
eastern and central portions of the eastern Pacific.
* Formation chance through 48 hours...low...near 0 percent.
* Formation chance through 7 days...medium...60 percent.

South of Hawaii:
Showers and thunderstorms associated with a trough of low pressure
located well south of the Hawaiian Islands remain disorganized.
Environmental conditions appear conducive for some gradual
development of this system as it moves generally westward during the
next several days.
* Formation chance through 48 hours...low...near 0 percent.
* Formation chance through 7 days...low...20 percent.

$$
Forecaster Katz/Cangialosi
item.author: nhcwebmaster@noaa.gov (NHC Webmaster)

item.title: Summary for Tropical Storm Fausto (EP1/EP062026)
item.author: nhcwebmaster@noaa.gov (NHC Webmaster)
item.description: ...FAUSTO CONTINUES TO STRENGTHEN... ...EXPECTED TO BECOME A HURRICANE SOON... As of 8:00 AM PDT Mon Jul 20 the center of Fausto was located near 13.5, -116.4 with movement WNW at 13 mph. The minimum central pressure was 994 mb with maximum sustained winds of about 65 mph.

nhc:Cyclone.nhc:name: Fausto
nhc:Cyclone.nhc:headline: ...FAUSTO CONTINUES TO STRENGTHEN... ...EXPECTED TO BECOME A HURRICANE SOON...

item.title: Tropical Storm Fausto Public Advisory Number 7
item.description:
Issued at 800 AM PDT Mon Jul 20 2026 
000
WTPZ31 KNHC 201434
TCPEP1
 
BULLETIN
Tropical Storm Fausto Advisory Number   7
NWS National Hurricane Center Miami FL       EP062026
800 AM PDT Mon Jul 20 2026
 
...FAUSTO CONTINUES TO STRENGTHEN...
...EXPECTED TO BECOME A HURRICANE SOON...
 
 
SUMMARY OF 800 AM PDT...1500 UTC...INFORMATION
----------------------------------------------
LOCATION...13.5N 116.4W
ABOUT 775 MI...1250 KM SW OF THE SOUTHERN TIP OF BAJA CALIFORNIA
MAXIMUM SUSTAINED WINDS...65 MPH...100 KM/H
PRESENT MOVEMENT...WNW OR 295 DEGREES AT 13 MPH...20 KM/H
MINIMUM CENTRAL PRESSURE...994 MB...29.36 INCHES
 
 
WATCHES AND WARNINGS
--------------------
There are no coastal watches or warnings in effect.
 
 
DISCUSSION AND OUTLOOK
----------------------
At 800 AM PDT (1500 UTC), the center of Tropical Storm Fausto was
located near latitude 13.5 North, longitude 116.4 West. Fausto is
moving toward the west-northwest near 13 mph (20 km/h).  A decrease 
in forward speed and a motion toward the northwest is expected 
during the next day or two.  A turn back to the west-northwest is 
expected on Wednesday.
 
Maximum sustained winds have increased to near 65 mph (100 km/h) 
with higher gusts.  Steady to rapid strengthening is expected during 
the next couple of days, and Fausto is forecast to become a 
hurricane later today or tonight.
 
Tropical-storm-force winds extend outward up to 140 miles (220 km)
from the center.
 
The estimated minimum central pressure is 994 mb (29.36 inches).
 
 
HAZARDS AFFECTING LAND
----------------------
SURF:  Swells generated by Fausto are expected to reach the west
coast of the Baja California Peninsula on Wednesday, and persist for
a couple of days.  These swells are likely to cause life-threatening
surf and rip current conditions.  Please consult products from your
local weather office.
 
 
NEXT ADVISORY
-------------
Next complete advisory at 200 PM PDT.
 
$$
Forecaster Cangialosi
item.author: nhcwebmaster@noaa.gov (NHC Webmaster)

item.title: Tropical Storm Fausto Forecast Advisory Number 7
item.description:
Issued at 1500 UTC MON JUL 20 2026 
768 
WTPZ21 KNHC 201433
TCMEP1
 
TROPICAL STORM FAUSTO FORECAST/ADVISORY NUMBER   7
NWS NATIONAL HURRICANE CENTER MIAMI FL       EP062026
1500 UTC MON JUL 20 2026
 
TROPICAL STORM CENTER LOCATED NEAR 13.5N 116.4W AT 20/1500Z
POSITION ACCURATE WITHIN  30 NM
 
PRESENT MOVEMENT TOWARD THE WEST-NORTHWEST OR 295 DEGREES AT  11 KT
 
ESTIMATED MINIMUM CENTRAL PRESSURE  994 MB
MAX SUSTAINED WINDS  55 KT WITH GUSTS TO  65 KT.
50 KT.......  0NE  40SE   0SW   0NW.
34 KT....... 80NE 120SE 120SW   0NW.
4 M SEAS....150NE 150SE  90SW  75NW.
WINDS AND SEAS VARY GREATLY IN EACH QUADRANT.  RADII IN NAUTICAL
MILES ARE THE LARGEST RADII EXPECTED ANYWHERE IN THAT QUADRANT.
 
REPEAT...CENTER LOCATED NEAR 13.5N 116.4W AT 20/1500Z
AT 20/1200Z CENTER WAS LOCATED NEAR 13.2N 116.0W
 
FORECAST VALID 21/0000Z 14.4N 117.5W
MAX WIND  65 KT...GUSTS  75 KT.
64 KT... 15NE  15SE   0SW   0NW.
50 KT... 40NE  40SE   0SW  20NW.
34 KT...120NE 150SE 140SW  60NW.
 
FORECAST VALID 21/1200Z 15.4N 119.0W
MAX WIND  75 KT...GUSTS  90 KT.
64 KT... 20NE  10SE   0SW  10NW.
50 KT... 40NE  50SE  20SW  30NW.
34 KT...140NE 160SE 130SW  70NW.
 
FORECAST VALID 22/0000Z 16.1N 120.6W
MAX WIND  85 KT...GUSTS 105 KT.
64 KT... 25NE  20SE   0SW  20NW.
50 KT... 50NE  60SE  30SW  40NW.
34 KT...140NE 160SE 110SW  90NW.
 
FORECAST VALID 22/1200Z 16.6N 122.1W
MAX WIND  95 KT...GUSTS 115 KT.
64 KT... 30NE  30SE  15SW  30NW.
50 KT... 60NE  70SE  40SW  50NW.
34 KT...140NE 150SE 110SW 100NW.
 
FORECAST VALID 23/0000Z 16.9N 124.0W
MAX WIND  95 KT...GUSTS 115 KT.
64 KT... 40NE  40SE  20SW  40NW.
50 KT... 70NE  70SE  40SW  60NW.
34 KT...150NE 150SE 110SW 120NW.
 
FORECAST VALID 23/1200Z 17.3N 126.3W
MAX WIND  90 KT...GUSTS 110 KT.
64 KT... 40NE  30SE  20SW  30NW.
50 KT... 70NE  70SE  40SW  60NW.
34 KT...150NE 140SE 110SW 130NW.
 
EXTENDED OUTLOOK. NOTE...ERRORS FOR TRACK HAVE AVERAGED NEAR 100 NM
ON DAY 4 AND 125 NM ON DAY 5...AND FOR INTENSITY NEAR 15 KT EACH DAY
 
OUTLOOK VALID 24/1200Z 18.0N 131.5W
MAX WIND  80 KT...GUSTS 100 KT.
50 KT... 70NE  60SE  40SW  60NW.
34 KT...150NE 140SE 110SW 140NW.
 
OUTLOOK VALID 25/1200Z 19.1N 136.7W
MAX WIND  70 KT...GUSTS  85 KT.
50 KT... 60NE  50SE  40SW  50NW.
34 KT...150NE 120SE 110SW 140NW.
 
REQUEST FOR 3 HOURLY SHIP REPORTS WITHIN 300 MILES OF 13.5N 116.4W
 
NEXT ADVISORY AT 20/2100Z
 
$$
FORECASTER CANGIALOSI
item.author: nhcwebmaster@noaa.gov (NHC Webmaster)

item.title: Tropical Storm Fausto Forecast Discussion Number 6
item.description:
Issued at 200 AM PDT Mon Jul 20 2026 
041 
WTPZ41 KNHC 200833
TCDEP1
 
Tropical Storm Fausto Discussion Number   6
NWS National Hurricane Center Miami FL       EP062026
200 AM PDT Mon Jul 20 2026
 
Although modest northerly vertical wind shear continues to impart a 
slight tilt to the vortex, a well-established upper-level outflow 
over the southern semicircle has allowed the convective organization 
to improve over the past few hours. As a result, the low-level 
circulation center remains located near the northern edge of the 
deepest convection, which was confirmed by a recent scatterometer 
pass. The scatterometer data also sampled peak surface winds of 
around 45 kt in the southeastern quadrant of the cyclone, which is 
consistent with the subjective Dvorak intensity estimate from TAFB. 
Based on the slightly improved satellite presentation and the recent 
scatterometer data, the initial intensity is raised to 50 kt.
 
The initial motion estimate is toward the west-northwest, or 290/10 
kt. A strong subtropical ridge is forecast to build westward in the 
wake of Elida, steering Fausto on a west-northwestward track during 
the next couple of days. As the ridge strengthens and expands 
westward, the cyclone is expected to gradually slow and bend 
slightly more westward through Wednesday. Thereafter, Fausto should 
resume a west-northwestward motion while accelerating later in the 
forecast period. The NHC track forecast is very similar to the 
previous advisory and lies near the multi-model consensus (HCCA) and 
the Google DeepMind ensemble mean.
 
Although modest northerly shear is likely contributing to Fausto's 
asymmetric structure, the overall environment remains conducive for 
strengthening, with warm sea surface temperatures, deep moisture, 
and improving upper-level conditions. Steady strengthening is 
expected today, followed by a period of rapid intensification 
forecast over the next couple of days, with Fausto forecast to 
become a hurricane within the next 24 hours. The cyclone is expected 
to reach its peak intensity in about 60 hours or around midweek. 
Thereafter, guidance suggest that decreasing upper-level divergence 
and a somewhat drier mid-level environment should induce a gradual 
weakening trend. The latest NHC intensity forecast remains near the 
middle of the guidance envelope through about 60 h, then lies near 
the upper end of the guidance envelope thereafter.
 
 
FORECAST POSITIONS AND MAX WINDS
 
INIT  20/0900Z 12.9N 115.6W   50 KT  60 MPH
 12H  20/1800Z 13.8N 116.9W   55 KT  65 MPH
 24H  21/0600Z 15.0N 118.3W   70 KT  80 MPH
 36H  21/1800Z 15.9N 119.9W   80 KT  90 MPH
 48H  22/0600Z 16.4N 121.5W   90 KT 105 MPH
 60H  22/1800Z 16.9N 123.1W   95 KT 110 MPH
 72H  23/0600Z 17.3N 125.2W   90 KT 105 MPH
 96H  24/0600Z 17.9N 130.4W   85 KT 100 MPH
120H  25/0600Z 18.8N 135.9W   75 KT  85 MPH
 
$$
Forecaster Gibbs (CPHC)
item.author: nhcwebmaster@noaa.gov (NHC Webmaster)

item.title: Tropical Storm Fausto Wind Speed Probabilities Number 6
item.description:
Issued at 0900 UTC MON JUL 20 2026 
000
FOPZ11 KNHC 200833
PWSEP1
                                                                    
TROPICAL STORM FAUSTO WIND SPEED PROBABILITIES NUMBER   6           
NWS NATIONAL HURRICANE CENTER MIAMI FL       EP062026               
0900 UTC MON JUL 20 2026                                            
                                                                    
AT 0900Z THE CENTER OF TROPICAL STORM FAUSTO WAS LOCATED NEAR       
LATITUDE 12.9 NORTH...LONGITUDE 115.6 WEST WITH MAXIMUM SUSTAINED   
WINDS NEAR 50 KTS...60 MPH...95 KM/H.                               
                                                                    
Z INDICATES COORDINATED UNIVERSAL TIME (GREENWICH)                  
   PACIFIC  DAYLIGHT TIME (PDT)...SUBTRACT 7 HOURS FROM Z TIME      
   HAWAIIAN STANDARD TIME (HST)...SUBTRACT 10 HOURS FROM Z TIME     
                                                                    
WIND SPEED PROBABILITY TABLE FOR SPECIFIC LOCATIONS                 
                                                                    
CHANCES OF SUSTAINED (1-MINUTE AVERAGE) WIND SPEEDS OF AT LEAST     
   ...34 KT (39 MPH... 63 KM/H)...                                  
   ...50 KT (58 MPH... 93 KM/H)...                                  
   ...64 KT (74 MPH...119 KM/H)...                                  
FOR LOCATIONS AND TIME PERIODS DURING THE NEXT 5 DAYS               
                                                                    
PROBABILITIES FOR LOCATIONS ARE GIVEN AS OP(CP) WHERE               
    OP  IS THE PROBABILITY OF THE EVENT BEGINNING DURING            
        AN INDIVIDUAL TIME PERIOD (ONSET PROBABILITY)               
   (CP) IS THE PROBABILITY OF THE EVENT OCCURRING BETWEEN           
        06Z MON AND THE FORECAST HOUR (CUMULATIVE PROBABILITY)      
                                                                    
PROBABILITIES ARE GIVEN IN PERCENT                                  
X INDICATES PROBABILITIES LESS THAN 1 PERCENT                       
PROBABILITIES FOR 34 KT AND 50 KT ARE SHOWN AT A GIVEN LOCATION WHEN
THE 5-DAY CUMULATIVE PROBABILITY IS AT LEAST 3 PERCENT.             
PROBABILITIES FOR 34...50...64 KT SHOWN WHEN THE 5-DAY              
64-KT CUMULATIVE PROBABILITY IS AT LEAST 1 PERCENT.                 
                                                                    
                                                                    
  - - - - WIND SPEED PROBABILITIES FOR SELECTED LOCATIONS - - - -   
                                                                    
               FROM    FROM    FROM    FROM    FROM    FROM    FROM 
  TIME       06Z MON 18Z MON 06Z TUE 18Z TUE 06Z WED 06Z THU 06Z FRI
PERIODS         TO      TO      TO      TO      TO      TO      TO  
             18Z MON 06Z TUE 18Z TUE 06Z WED 06Z THU 06Z FRI 06Z SAT
                                                                    
FORECAST HOUR    (12)   (24)    (36)    (48)    (72)    (96)   (120)
- - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - 
LOCATION       KT                                                   
                                                                    
15N 115W       34  5   2( 7)   X( 7)   X( 7)   X( 7)   X( 7)   X( 7)
 
15N 120W       34  X  23(23)  53(76)   3(79)   X(79)   X(79)   X(79)
15N 120W       50  X   X( X)  25(25)   3(28)   X(28)   X(28)   X(28)
15N 120W       64  X   X( X)   9( 9)   3(12)   X(12)   X(12)   X(12)
 
20N 120W       34  X   X( X)   X( X)   2( 2)   1( 3)   X( 3)   X( 3)
 
15N 125W       34  X   X( X)   X( X)   1( 1)  13(14)   2(16)   X(16)
15N 125W       50  X   X( X)   X( X)   X( X)   2( 2)   X( 2)   X( 2)
15N 125W       64  X   X( X)   X( X)   X( X)   1( 1)   X( 1)   X( 1)
 
20N 125W       34  X   X( X)   X( X)   X( X)  19(19)   4(23)   X(23)
20N 125W       50  X   X( X)   X( X)   X( X)   2( 2)   X( 2)   1( 3)

15N 130W       50  X   X( X)   X( X)   X( X)   X( X)   2( 2)   X( 2)
15N 130W       64  X   X( X)   X( X)   X( X)   X( X)   1( 1)   X( 1)
 
20N 130W       34  X   X( X)   X( X)   X( X)   X( X)  40(40)   3(43)
20N 130W       50  X   X( X)   X( X)   X( X)   X( X)  10(10)   3(13)
20N 130W       64  X   X( X)   X( X)   X( X)   X( X)   4( 4)   2( 6)
 
15N 135W       34  X   X( X)   X( X)   X( X)   X( X)   1( 1)   7( 8)
 
20N 135W       34  X   X( X)   X( X)   X( X)   X( X)   3( 3)  53(56)
20N 135W       50  X   X( X)   X( X)   X( X)   X( X)   X( X)  24(24)
20N 135W       64  X   X( X)   X( X)   X( X)   X( X)   X( X)  13(13)
 
20N 140W       34  X   X( X)   X( X)   X( X)   X( X)   X( X)  15(15)
20N 140W       50  X   X( X)   X( X)   X( X)   X( X)   X( X)   3( 3)
20N 140W       64  X   X( X)   X( X)   X( X)   X( X)   X( X)   1( 1)
 
$$                                                                  
FORECASTER GIBBS (CPHC)

item.title: Tropical Storm Fausto Graphics
item.description: Wind Speed Probabilities last updated Mon, 20 Jul 2026 09:33:43 GMT
item.author: nhcwebmaster@noaa.gov (NHC Webmaster)

item.title: Summary for Post-Tropical Cyclone Elida (EP5/EP052026)
item.author: nhcwebmaster@noaa.gov (NHC Webmaster)
item.description: ...ELIDA BECOMES POST-TROPICAL... ...DANGEROUS SURF AND HIGH RISK OF RIP CURRENTS IN SOUTHERN CALIFORNIA CONTINUES... As of 8:00 AM PDT Mon Jul 20 the center of Elida was located near 26.4, -127.2 with movement NNW at 13 mph. The minimum central pressure was 1000 mb with maximum sustained winds of about 40 mph.

nhc:Cyclone.nhc:name: Elida
nhc:Cyclone.nhc:headline: ...ELIDA BECOMES POST-TROPICAL... ...DANGEROUS SURF AND HIGH RISK OF RIP CURRENTS IN SOUTHERN CALIFORNIA CONTINUES...

item.title: Post-Tropical Cyclone Elida Public Advisory Number 24
item.description:
Issued at 800 AM PDT Mon Jul 20 2026 
ZCZC MIATCPEP5 ALL
TTAA00 KNHC DDHHMM
 
BULLETIN
Post-Tropical Cyclone Elida Advisory Number  24
NWS National Hurricane Center Miami FL       EP052026
800 AM PDT Mon Jul 20 2026
 
...ELIDA BECOMES POST-TROPICAL...
...DANGEROUS SURF AND HIGH RISK OF RIP CURRENTS IN SOUTHERN
CALIFORNIA CONTINUES...
 
 
SUMMARY OF 800 AM PDT...1500 UTC...INFORMATION
----------------------------------------------
LOCATION...26.4N 127.2W
ABOUT 1110 MI...1790 KM WNW OF THE SOUTHERN TIP OF BAJA CALIFORNIA
MAXIMUM SUSTAINED WINDS...40 MPH...65 KM/H
PRESENT MOVEMENT...NNW OR 345 DEGREES AT 13 MPH...20 KM/H
MINIMUM CENTRAL PRESSURE...1000 MB...29.53 INCHES
 
 
WATCHES AND WARNINGS
--------------------
There are no coastal watches or warnings in effect.
 
 
DISCUSSION AND OUTLOOK
----------------------
At 800 AM PDT (1500 UTC), the center of Post-Tropical Cyclone Elida 
was located near latitude 26.4 North, longitude 127.2 West. The 
post-tropical cyclone is moving toward the north-northwest near 13 
mph (20 km/h). A turn to the north with an increase in forward speed 
is forecast the during the next couple days.
 
Maximum sustained winds are near 40 mph (65 km/h) with higher gusts.
The post-tropical cyclone is expected to continue to weaken and 
should dissipate within the next couple of days.

Tropical-storm-force winds extend outward up to 140 miles (220 km)
from the center.
 
The estimated minimum central pressure is 1000 mb (29.53 inches).
 
 
HAZARDS AFFECTING LAND
----------------------
SURF:  Swells generated by Elida will affect portions of the west
coast of the Baja California Peninsula and southern California
during the next several days. These swells will likely result in
life-threatening surf and rip current conditions. Please consult
products from your local weather office.
 
 
NEXT ADVISORY
-------------
This is the last public advisory issued by the National Hurricane 
Center on this system. For additional information please see High 
Seas Forecasts issued by the National Weather Service, under AWIPS 
header NFDHSFEPI, WMO header FZPN02 KWBC, and on the web at 
ocean.weather.gov/shtml/NFDHSFEPI.php

$$
Forecaster Katz/Cangialosi
 
NNNN
item.author: nhcwebmaster@noaa.gov (NHC Webmaster)

item.title: Post-Tropical Cyclone Elida Forecast Advisory Number 24
item.description:
Issued at 1500 UTC MON JUL 20 2026 
000
WTPZ25 KNHC 201434
TCMEP5
 
POST-TROPICAL CYCLONE ELIDA FORECAST/ADVISORY NUMBER  24
NWS NATIONAL HURRICANE CENTER MIAMI FL       EP052026
1500 UTC MON JUL 20 2026
 
POST-TROPICAL CYCLONE CENTER LOCATED NEAR 26.4N 127.2W AT 20/1500Z
POSITION ACCURATE WITHIN  30 NM
 
PRESENT MOVEMENT TOWARD THE NORTH-NORTHWEST OR 345 DEGREES AT  11 KT
 
ESTIMATED MINIMUM CENTRAL PRESSURE 1000 MB
MAX SUSTAINED WINDS  35 KT WITH GUSTS TO  45 KT.
34 KT.......120NE  40SE   0SW   0NW.
4 M SEAS....180NE 180SE 300SW 180NW.
WINDS AND SEAS VARY GREATLY IN EACH QUADRANT.  RADII IN NAUTICAL
MILES ARE THE LARGEST RADII EXPECTED ANYWHERE IN THAT QUADRANT.
 
REPEAT...CENTER LOCATED NEAR 26.4N 127.2W AT 20/1500Z
AT 20/1200Z CENTER WAS LOCATED NEAR 25.7N 127.1W
 
FORECAST VALID 21/0000Z 28.5N 127.4W...POST-TROPICAL
MAX WIND  35 KT...GUSTS  45 KT.
34 KT... 80NE   0SE   0SW  70NW.
 
FORECAST VALID 21/1200Z 31.1N 127.6W...POST-TROP/REMNT LOW
MAX WIND  30 KT...GUSTS  40 KT.
 
FORECAST VALID 22/0000Z 33.6N 127.4W...POST-TROP/REMNT LOW
MAX WIND  25 KT...GUSTS  35 KT.
 
FORECAST VALID 22/1200Z 35.6N 127.1W...POST-TROP/REMNT LOW
MAX WIND  20 KT...GUSTS  30 KT.
 
FORECAST VALID 23/0000Z 37.7N 126.6W...POST-TROP/REMNT LOW
MAX WIND  15 KT...GUSTS  20 KT.
 
FORECAST VALID 23/1200Z...DISSIPATED
 
REQUEST FOR 3 HOURLY SHIP REPORTS WITHIN 300 MILES OF 26.4N 127.2W
 
THIS IS THE LAST FORECAST/ADVISORY ISSUED BY THE NATIONAL HURRICANE 
CENTER ON THIS SYSTEM. FOR ADDITIONAL INFORMATION PLEASE SEE HIGH 
SEAS FORECASTS ISSUED BY THE NATIONAL WEATHER SERVICE...UNDER AWIPS 
HEADER NFDHSFEPI AND WMO HEADER FZPN02 KWBC.
 
$$
FORECASTER KATZ/CANGIALOSI
item.author: nhcwebmaster@noaa.gov (NHC Webmaster)

item.title: Tropical Storm Elida Forecast Discussion Number 23
item.description:
Issued at 200 AM PDT Mon Jul 20 2026 
690 
WTPZ45 KNHC 200848
TCDEP5
 
Tropical Storm Elida Discussion Number  23
NWS National Hurricane Center Miami FL       EP052026
200 AM PDT Mon Jul 20 2026
 
Satellite imagery shows convection associated with Elida continues 
to fade as the cyclone encounters cooler sea surface temperatures, 
drier air, and increasing southwesterly wind shear. A recent partial 
ASCAT-C pass indicates the system still has winds of around 42 kt, 
so the initial intensity is held at 45 kt for this advisory.
 
Gradual weakening is expected through the forecast period as the 
hostile environmental conditions persist, with the cyclone forecast 
to degenerate into a remnant low and then eventually dissipate into 
an open trough in a few days. The latest TAFB satellite intensity 
estimate was too weak to classify because of the lack of persistent 
deep convection, suggesting that Elida could degenerate into a 
post-tropical cyclone sooner than currently forecast if these 
satellite trends continue.
 
Elida has turned toward the north-northwest and appears to be 
increasing its forward speed, with the latest motion estimated at 
340/11 kt. This north-northwestward then northward motion should 
continue the next few days as the cyclone moves between a deep-layer 
trough to its northwest and a ridge centered over the southwestern 
United States. The NHC track forecast is near the previous, and lies 
near the corrected consensus HCCA.
  
FORECAST POSITIONS AND MAX WINDS
 
INIT  20/0900Z 25.3N 127.1W   45 KT  50 MPH
 12H  20/1800Z 27.2N 127.5W   40 KT  45 MPH
 24H  21/0600Z 30.0N 127.8W   35 KT  40 MPH...POST-TROPICAL
 36H  21/1800Z 32.7N 127.7W   30 KT  35 MPH...POST-TROP/REMNT LOW
 48H  22/0600Z 34.6N 127.4W   25 KT  30 MPH...POST-TROP/REMNT LOW
 60H  22/1800Z 36.7N 127.0W   20 KT  25 MPH...POST-TROP/REMNT LOW
 72H  23/0600Z...DISSIPATED
 
$$
Forecaster Kelly
item.author: nhcwebmaster@noaa.gov (NHC Webmaster)

item.title: Tropical Storm Elida Wind Speed Probabilities Number 23
item.description:
Issued at 0900 UTC MON JUL 20 2026 
663 
FOPZ15 KNHC 200847
PWSEP5
                                                                    
TROPICAL STORM ELIDA WIND SPEED PROBABILITIES NUMBER  23            
NWS NATIONAL HURRICANE CENTER MIAMI FL       EP052026               
0900 UTC MON JUL 20 2026                                            
                                                                    
AT 0900Z THE CENTER OF TROPICAL STORM ELIDA WAS LOCATED NEAR        
LATITUDE 25.3 NORTH...LONGITUDE 127.1 WEST WITH MAXIMUM SUSTAINED   
WINDS NEAR 45 KTS...50 MPH...85 KM/H.                               
                                                                    
Z INDICATES COORDINATED UNIVERSAL TIME (GREENWICH)                  
   PACIFIC  DAYLIGHT TIME (PDT)...SUBTRACT 7 HOURS FROM Z TIME      
   HAWAIIAN STANDARD TIME (HST)...SUBTRACT 10 HOURS FROM Z TIME     
                                                                    
WIND SPEED PROBABILITY TABLE FOR SPECIFIC LOCATIONS                 
                                                                    
CHANCES OF SUSTAINED (1-MINUTE AVERAGE) WIND SPEEDS OF AT LEAST     
   ...34 KT (39 MPH... 63 KM/H)...                                  
   ...50 KT (58 MPH... 93 KM/H)...                                  
   ...64 KT (74 MPH...119 KM/H)...                                  
FOR LOCATIONS AND TIME PERIODS DURING THE NEXT 5 DAYS               
                                                                    
PROBABILITIES FOR LOCATIONS ARE GIVEN AS OP(CP) WHERE               
    OP  IS THE PROBABILITY OF THE EVENT BEGINNING DURING            
        AN INDIVIDUAL TIME PERIOD (ONSET PROBABILITY)               
   (CP) IS THE PROBABILITY OF THE EVENT OCCURRING BETWEEN           
        06Z MON AND THE FORECAST HOUR (CUMULATIVE PROBABILITY)      
                                                                    
PROBABILITIES ARE GIVEN IN PERCENT                                  
X INDICATES PROBABILITIES LESS THAN 1 PERCENT                       
PROBABILITIES FOR 34 KT AND 50 KT ARE SHOWN AT A GIVEN LOCATION WHEN
THE 5-DAY CUMULATIVE PROBABILITY IS AT LEAST 3 PERCENT.             
PROBABILITIES FOR 34...50...64 KT SHOWN WHEN THE 5-DAY              
64-KT CUMULATIVE PROBABILITY IS AT LEAST 1 PERCENT.                 
                                                                    
                                                                    
  - - - - WIND SPEED PROBABILITIES FOR SELECTED LOCATIONS - - - -   
                                                                    
               FROM    FROM    FROM    FROM    FROM    FROM    FROM 
  TIME       06Z MON 18Z MON 06Z TUE 18Z TUE 06Z WED 06Z THU 06Z FRI
PERIODS         TO      TO      TO      TO      TO      TO      TO  
             18Z MON 06Z TUE 18Z TUE 06Z WED 06Z THU 06Z FRI 06Z SAT
                                                                    
FORECAST HOUR    (12)   (24)    (36)    (48)    (72)    (96)   (120)
- - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - - 
LOCATION       KT                                                   
                                                                    
$$                                                                  
FORECASTER KELLY
item.author: nhcwebmaster@noaa.gov (NHC Webmaster)
